info.title: Duck Creek Payments API
info.description: Duck Creek Payments Orchestrator API (formerly Imburse Payments). Provides authentication, configuration, and transaction management for payment processing in the Duck Creek insurance platform.

post.description:
### Role Requirements
`tenant-transaction-write` or `tenant-admin`
### Description
Upload a batch of instructions.

requestBody.description: The batch of instructions to upload that are ready for scheduling

file.description:
The file containing the batch of ready to schedule instructions.

The file should be a CSV of instructions, up to 10,000 instructions, where each instruction is an object with the following properties:

- `orderRef` (required, string, `<= 50 characters`, `^[a-zA-Z0-9-_\.]+$`): A unique reference for the order
- `instructionRef` (required, string, `<= 50 characters`, `^[a-zA-Z0-9-_\.]+$`): A unique reference for the instruction within the order
- `customerRef` (required, string, `<= 50 characters`, `^[a-zA-Z0-9-_\.]+$`): A reference for the customer
- `direction` (required, string, `DEBIT|CREDIT`): The direction of the instruction
- `financialInstrumentId` (GUID): The ID of the financial instrument
- `amount` (required, string): The amount of the instruction
- `currency` (required, string, `3 characters`): The ISO 4217 currency code of the instruction
- `country` (required, string, `2 characters`): The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the instruction
- `settledByDate` (required, date): The date by which the instruction should be settled
- `schemeId` (String, 32 characters): The ID of the scheme
- `metadata.*` (string, up to 10 keys): Additional metadata for the instruction. Keys have a max length of 64 characters. Values have a max length of 256 characters.

**NOTE**: If you are considering using metadata, please talk to us first to ensure that the metadata you are considering is appropriate and being used efficiently.

### File example:
```csv
orderRef,instructionRef,customerRef,direction,financialInstrumentId,amount,currency,country,settledByDate,schemeId,metadata.category,metadata.priority
ORDER001,INST001,CUST001,DEBIT,4dc3291b-271d-4610-adb3-29716e2ff00b,100.50,GBP,GB,2025-02-19,4D5768FA897089DC2AAEDC0EBE6F46E2,Utility,Basic
ORDER002,INST002,CUST002,DEBIT,25bf4e87-2a72-4f79-86be-de32f4594a32,200.75,GBP,GB,2025-02-20,4D5768FA897089DC2AAEDC0EBE6F46E2,Personal,Plus
ORDER003,INST003,CUST003,DEBIT,16c064b7-ab4d-4d37-8396-d2fb15d489e7,300.80,GBP,GB,2025-02-21,4D5768FA897089DC2AAEDC0EBE6F46E2,,Basic
ORDER004,INST004,CUST004,DEBIT,8ccfd0c2-3801-4714-a046-ab900c22e01e,400.95,GBP,GB,2025-02-22,4D5768FA897089DC2AAEDC0EBE6F46E2,,
ORDER005,INST005,CUST005,DEBIT,420069eb-4432-4d4c-b4d3-5f0b465672ae,500.10,GBP,GB,2025-02-23,4D5768FA897089DC2AAEDC0EBE6F46E2,Utility,
```

post.description:
### Role Requirements
`tenant-transaction-write` or `tenant-admin`
### Description
Upload a batch of reimbursements.

requestBody.description: The batch of reimbursements to upload

file.description:
The file containing the batch reimbursements.

The file should be a CSV of reimbursements, up to 10,000 rows, where each reimbursement is a row with the following properties:

- `orderRef` (required, string, `<= 50 characters`, `^[a-zA-Z0-9-_\.]+$`): A unique reference for the order
- `instructionRef` (required, string, `<= 50 characters`, `^[a-zA-Z0-9-_\.]+$`): A unique reference for the instruction within the order
- `reimbursementRef` (optional, string, `<= 50 characters`, `^[a-zA-Z0-9-_\.]+$`): A unique reference for the reimbursement for an instruction
- `amount` (required, string): The amount of the instruction
- `reason` (optional, string, `<= 100 characters`): A reason for the reimbursement

### File example:
```csv
orderRef,instructionRef,reimbursementRef,amount,reason
ORDER001,INST001,REIMBURSE001,100.50,"Product defect or damage"
ORDER002,INST002,,200.75,
ORDER003,INST003,REIMBURSE003,300.80,"Item not as described"
ORDER004,INST004,REIMBURSE004,400.95,"Expired or perishable item"
ORDER005,INST005,,500.10,"Overcharge or billing error"
```

The ability to hide financial instruments that share the same core attributes.
The core attributes are different for each financial instrument type, as shown in the table below:
|                      | FI Type | Account Number | Extra Code | Account Type | Token |
|----------------------|---------|----------------|-----------|-------------|--------|
| Mandate - ACH        | ✓       | ✓             | ✓         | ✓           |        |
| Bank Account - US    | ✓       | ✓             | ✓         | ✓           |        |
| Mandate - SEPA       | ✓       | ✓             | ✓         |             |        |
| Bank Account - IBAN  | ✓       | ✓             | ✓         |             |        |

| Mandate - BECS AU    | ✓       | ✓             | ✓         |             |        |
| Bank Account - AU    | ✓       | ✓             | ✓         |             |        |
| Mandate - BECS NZ    | ✓       | ✓             |           |             |        |
| Bank Account - NZ    | ✓       | ✓             |           |             |        |
| Card Token           | ✓       |               |           |             | ✓     |

SourceFlow.description:

- **OnSession**: The financial instrument was created by customer via an On Session.

- **OffSession**: The financial instrument was added via the API by the client.

* Maximum of 15 keys.

* Values have a maximum length of 256 characters.

NetAmount.description:
The amount available for new Refunds, Chargebacks or other operations on the transaction. 
The number of decimal digits is validated against the currency code of the payment.

The amount is calculated as the difference between the original amount and the sum of all the completed Refunds and Chargebacks.

appId.description: The App Id used to execute the transaction.

appTransactionId.description: The transaction id used by the App.

appBatchId.description: The batch id used by the App.

supportDetails.description: Additional Information supplied by the App.

merchantOperatingAccount.description: Identifier of the merchant funding account to be used for this transaction, overriding the provider's default operating account configuration. For CREDIT transactions, this account is used as the source of funds. For DEBIT transactions, this account is used as the destination of funds. This value represents a provider-defined account identifier (not a customer bank account number) and must correspond to a funding account configured for the merchant. If not provided, the PSP's default operating account configuration will be used. If the PSP does not support funding account overrides, this field is ignored.

AppResponseV1.description: The App (PSP) used to execute the transaction.

status.description:
The status of the Refund. Can be one of:
  - Processing - The Refund was requested by the customer and is yet to be resolved.
  - Completed - The Refund was processed and completed.
  - Failed - The Refund was failed due to issues with the bank account or card, and the provider could not process it.

failureReasonCode.description: The reason code for the refund failure.

failureDescription.description: The description of the refund failure.

network.description: The network where the Chargeback was generated.

status.description:
The status of the Chargeback. Can be one of:
- Pending - The Chargeback was claimed by the customer and is yet to be resolved.
- Completed - The Chargeback was either accepted by the collector, or unsuccessfully challenged.
- Cancelled - The Chargeback was challenged by the collector and subsequently withdrawn by the customer.

claimed.description: The date when the Chargeback was claimed by the customer.

created.description: The UTC date time when the Chargeback entity was created.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The UTC date time when the Chargeback entity was last updated.

payingBankReference.description: The bank reference for the chargeback.

reasonCode.description: The reason code for the chargeback request.

description.description: The description for the chargeback request.

channel.description:
The method of authorization used for the transaction.
- MOTO - Mail Order / Telephone Order.
- eCom - Online Transaction.
- PAPER - Non card Transaction, where it used a financial instrument with Authorization Source PAPER.
- TEL - Non card Transaction, where it used a financial instrument with Authorization Source TEL.

authenticationFlow.description:
The challenge type used during Strong Customer Authentication (SCA), when provided by the PSP.
- 3DS - There was 3DS on the transaction, but the PSP hasn't provided additional information on the authentication method.
- 3DS Challenge - There was 3DS on the transaction, and the 3DS authentication occurred via a challenge.
- 3DS Frictionless - There was 3DS on the transaction, and the 3DS authentication occurred via a frictionless flow.

status.description:
The status of the financial instrument (FI).

- **ACTIVE**: The FI is operational and can be used for payments and premiums.
- **INACTIVE**: The FI is not operational and cannot be used for payments or premiums. Inactivity reasons are recorded for clarity.
- **LOCKED**: The FI has been successfully used for a single-use payment and cannot be used for further transactions.
- **CLOSED**: The FI has been permanently closed by the Carrier and cannot be used for any payments or premiums.

Unique reference identifier for the creditor profile. 
It accepts alphanumeric characters (*a* to *z* or digits), as well as the symbols `-`, `_` and `.`.

MandateAuthorizationSource.description:
**Required for ACH only**

The authorization code indicates how the mandate was authorized.

This defaults to PPD for B2C and CCD for B2B

Credit direction only supports PPD and CCD

AuthorizationFrequency.description:
**Required for ACH only**

The authorization frequency indicates how often the mandate is authorized.

If set to `Open`, the mandate is authorized for use without a fixed or predefined frequency. This may apply to mandates where the timing of transactions is irregular, variable, or determined dynamically.

If set to `Other`, the `authorizationFrequencyOtherWording` field must be provided.

AuthorizationFrequencyOtherWording.description:
**Required for ACH only**

This is the wording displayed to the customer on the ACH Authorization screen during checkout flow, when the authorization frequency is set to `Other`.

It will be displayed within the following sentence: "To be collected [authorizationFrequencyOtherWording]."

If `authorizationFrequency` set to `Other`, then this field is required.

Value must be a string with a minimum length of 1 and a maximum length of 100 characters, and can contain only the following characters: lowercase letters, numbers, space, comma, parentheses, single quote, hyphen, ampersand, forward slash.
AuthorizationFrequencyOtherWording.example: quarterly for the first 3 installments, every month thereafter

accountNumber.description:
**BACS, BECS AU, BECS NZ, and ACH only**

The number of the account.

For BACS:
- 8 digits only. 12345678 is valid, but 00000000, 1234567 and 123456789 are invalid.

For BECS AU:
- Between 6 and 10 digits.

For BECS NZ:
- Between 15 and 16 digits.

For ACH:
- Between 6 and 17 digits.

sortCode.description:
**BACS only**

The 6 digits comprising an account's sort-code.
101010 is valid, but 000000, 1234567 and 12345 are invalid.

bsbNumber.description:
**BECS AU only**

BSB (Bank State Branch Number): BSB number, is a 6-digit code that
represents your bank, state, and branch location.

iban.description:
**SEPA only**

The International Bank Account Number. It's format is specified by
ISO 13616 and consists of the country code, 2 letters (ISO 3166), 2
check digits and up to 30 alphanumeric characters that represent the
Basic Bank Account Number.

bic.description:
**SEPA only**

The Bank Identifier Code, the SWIFT address assigned to the bank
that holds the account. It consists of a 4 character bank code, 2
character country code (ISO 3166), 2 character location code for the
bank's head office and an optional 3 character code for the branch
location. [Required for specific countries]

bankName.description:
**BECS NZ only**

The name of the account's bank.

signatoryName.description:
**BECS NZ only**

The account holder's signatory name.

accountType.description:
**ACH only**

The type of bank account, such as checking or savings.

This field is used to identify the account's purpose and determine applicable rules and restrictions.

routingNumber.description:
**ACH only**

The nine-digit ACH routing number associated with the bank account. This number is used to identify the financial institution where the account is held and is required for electronic funds transfers (EFT) such as ACH payments, direct deposits, and wire transfers.

addToWallet.description:

Indicates whether the mandate should be added to the consumer wallet.

ProviderNotificationPeriod.description:
Notification period in days
Advanced notice period: the advance notice is the notice to the Payer (the person paying the Direct Debit), before the first Direct Debit is collected or when any changes are made to the amount, collection date or frequency of the Direct Debit collection. The notice period can be amendment provided you have the agreement of your sponsoring bank.

The minimum notice period is 3 days for SEPA and BACS, 1 day for BECS AU and 2 days for BECS NZ.

WLSAuthorizationFrequencyOtherWording.description:
**Required for ACH only**

**If `authorizationFrequency` set to `Other`, then this field is required**

This is the wording displayed to the customer on the ACH Authorization screen during checkout flow, when the authorization frequency is set to `Other`.

Value must be a string with a minimum length of 1 and a maximum length of 100 characters, and can contain only the following characters: lowercase letters, numbers, space, comma, parentheses, single quote, hyphen, ampersand, forward slash.

It will be displayed within the following sentence: "To be collected [authorizationFrequencyOtherWording]."

ChargebackStatus.description:
Status of the Chargeback. Can be one of:

* `Pending` - The Chargeback was claimed by the customer and is yet to be resolved.
* `Completed` - The Chargeback was either accepted by the collector, or unsuccessfully challenged.
* `Cancelled` - The Chargeback was challenged by the collector and subsequently withdrawn by the customer.
